title: Cross the JS boundary through generated bindings
description: Pass strings, structs, and arrays via a binding layer (wasm-bindgen) that marshals them, not as raw integers you decode by hand.
body: Use when exposing functions across the JS<->WASM boundary. Do not hand-pass pointers and lengths and decode linear memory manually unless you must, because the ABI only natively carries numbers and getting the encoding/ownership wrong corrupts data. Actions: use wasm-bindgen (or the component model) to generate typed glue that copies strings/arrays in and out and manages allocation, and keep the raw exported functions for hot numeric paths only. Validation signals include round-tripping a string and a struct across the boundary and getting identical values back in a test. Risk boundary: a hand-rolled ABI that mis-encodes length or ownership reads garbage or frees twice; prefer generated bindings and verify any manual marshalling with a round-trip test.
